perturbation.original_text: All payments by End-Users for Products shall be made directly to Distributor, and Distributor shall so advise Developer of the End-Users to whom sales are made.
perturbation.changed_text: All payments by End-Users for Products shall be made as per the discretion of Distributor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that End-Users make payments directly to the Distributor. The modified text now states that payments are made as per the Distributor's discretion which is contradictory because the original contract states that it should be made directly to the distributor. This causes an in-text contradiction, as it introduces uncertainty and conflicts with the initial arrangement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (g) All payments by End-Users for Products shall be made as per the discretion of Distributor.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that payments must be made directly to the distributor, while the changed text grants the distributor discretion over how payments are made. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, stating payments are made as per the discretion of distributor. This creates a direct contradiction to the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Developer retains the right, in its sole discretion, to upgrade or modify the Products from time to time. In addition, upon ninety (90) days prior written notice to Distributor, Developer may add or delete Products from Schedule A. Upon receipt of any such notice of any upgrade or modification, or upon the expiration of the notice period set forth above for additions or deletions to Schedule A, Distributor shall cease to market and distribute earlier versions of the Products and/or Products deleted from Schedule A.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer retains the right to upgrade or modify the Products from time to time. Distributor shall cease to market and distribute earlier versions of the Products and/or Products deleted from Schedule A.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'In addition, upon ninety (90) days prior written notice to Distributor, Developer may add or delete Products from Schedule A. Upon receipt of any such notice of any upgrade or modification, or upon the expiration of the notice period set forth above for additions or deletions to Schedule A' creates an in-text contradiction. While the original text specifies a 90-day notice for adding or deleting Products from Schedule A, the edited version removes this obligation. This contradicts any expectation that the Distributor would be informed about changes in the Schedule with prior notice. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Developer retains the right to upgrade or modify the Products from time to time. Distributor shall cease to market and distribute earlier versions of the Products and/or Products deleted from Schedule A.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the 90-day notice period for adding or deleting products from Schedule A in the changed text directly contradicts the expectation set by the contradicted text that Distributor would be informed about changes in the Schedule with prior notice. The original text specified a 90-day notice for changes to Schedule A, while the changed text removes this obligation entirely, which creates a direct conflict regarding the procedure for making changes to Schedule A.
